data.title: Avenira secures $7.56m investment from majority shareholder Hebang to advance Wonarah phosphate project
data.description: Avenira has secured a $7.56 million strategic investment from majority shareholder Hebang Biotechnology to progress its Wonarah phosphate project in the Northern Territory. The investment, in which Hebang will acquire 1.08 billion shares priced at $0.007 each, will boost its equity holding in Avenira to 49%. Hebang has also agreed to provide Avenira with an unsecured drawdown loan facility of $7.56m to be repaid on completion of the placement or after the date of the first drawdown. Advancing Wonarah Avenira will use funds from the investment to advance its Wonarah phosphate project, which is located between Tennant Creek and Mount Isa and has a resource base of 812 million tonnes. Wonarah is considered Australia’s largest high-grade phosphate project and Avenira plans to develop it as a direct shipping ore (DSO) operation based on a simple open-cut mining operation with processing facilities onsite. Avenira intends to supply premium-quality products from Wonarah—including DSO, lithium ferro phosphate (LFP), thermal phosphoric acid (TPA) and yellow phosphorus—into the electric vehicle, agricultural and industrial chemical markets. Initial placement The latest investment follows an initial $4.5m two-tranche placement to Hebang in July, via the issue of a total of 750 million Avenira shares at $0.006 each. Hebang also provided a $2.79m unsecured loan facility in September, to be repaid from placement funds. That investment gave Avenira the opportunity to accelerate the downstream development of yellow phosphorus and deliver a key element in the production of LFP for next-generation batteries. Principal shareholder Avenira deputy chair Brett Clark welcomed the latest strategic investment by Hebang, which is a wholly owned subsidiary of Sichuan Hebang Biotechnology Corporation. “We are delighted that our principal shareholder has chosen [to make] a further investment in Avenira,” he said. “This enhanced co-operation with Hebang provides us with the opportunity to accelerate Wonarah towards the targeted delivery of ore to port before year-end and substantially enhance the value of the significant resource.” Phased development Avenira’s board announced in February that it would prioritise the Wonarah project with a phased development strategy focused on disciplined capital allocation and sustainable growth. Key phases of the strategy would include early revenue generation from a Stage 2 DSO operation and completion of a feasibility study for a Stage 3 operation aimed at unlocking Wonarah’s economic potential by upgrading to commercial-grade products, enhancing scalability and viability. “This strategic shift will allow the board and management to refocus their efforts on a new development pathway that provides Avenira the opportunity for early revenue generation in the second half of 2025,” Mr Clark said.

data.title: Market Chatter: US Considers Ban on Chinese AI Chatbot DeepSeek Over Data Security Concerns
data.description:
The US is weighing a ban on Chinese AI chatbot DeepSeek from government devices, with US officials alarmed by DeepSeek's handling of user data being stored on servers in China, Reuters reported Friday.

The administration is also exploring measures to remove the chatbot from app stores and restrict US cloud providers from offering its AI models, according to the report. These discussions are still reportedly in early stages.

The move follows a push from 21 state attorneys general urging Congress to prohibit the chatbot on government devices, Reuters wrote.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Air New Zealand Buys Back Over 365,000 Ordinary Shares
data.description:
Air New Zealand acquired 365,466 ordinary shares as part of its on-market and off-market share buyback program disclosed last Feb. 20, according to a Monday filing with the Australian and New Zealand bourses.

The acquisition comprised 179,079 ordinary shares bought on the NZX and the ASX, and 186,387 ordinary shares bought off-market from New Zealand's Ministry of Finance, per the filing.

On average, the company paid NZ$0.595 per share acquired on the NZX; AU$0.5423 per share acquired on the ASX; and NZ$0.5972 per share acquired from the ministry.

data.title: Life360's Australia-listed shares gain after addition to benchmark index, Johns Lyng falls
data.description:
** Location tech firm Life360's Australia-listed shares rise 3.4% to A$22.98, while builder Johns Lyng Group falls 9.3% to A$2.54

** S&P Dow Jones Indices on Friday removed Johns Lyng Group from the benchmark S&P/ASX 200 index and added Life360

** JLG biggest loser in the benchmark index, 360 among top gainers

** JLG stock down 32.1% YTD, while 360 up 2.2%

data.title: Toyota Says Production At 4 Lines At 3 Plants In Japan Will Be Halted During 2Nd Shift On March 10 And 1St Shift On March 11 Due To Accident At Supplier Chuo Spring
data.description:
Chuo Spring Co Ltd :

  • TOYOTA SAYS PRODUCTION AT 4 LINES AT 3 PLANTS IN JAPAN WILL BE HALTED DURING 2ND SHIFT ON MARCH 10 AND 1ST SHIFT ON MARCH 11 DUE TO ACCIDENT AT SUPPLIER CHUO SPRING

  • TOYOTA SAYS AFFECTED LINES INCLUDE ONE OF TWO LINES AT TAKAOKA FACTORY, TWO LINES AT TOYOTA INDUSTRIES' NAGAKUSA PLANT AND ONE PRODUCTION LINE FOR TOYOTA VEHICLES AT DAIHATSU'S KYOTO PLANT

  • TOYOTA SAYS TOYOTA SAYS IT WILL DECIDE AROUND NOON ON MARCH 11 ABOUT FURTHER OPERATIONS AND THAT IT CAN'T PREDICT THE IMPACT ON PRODUCTION VOLUMES AT THIS STAGE

data.title: Palm Oil Rises on Slower Production Concerns — Market Talk
data.description: Palm oil prices rise in Asian trading, amid concerns over slower production in Malaysia's key growing areas, AmInvestment Bank says in a note. Expectations of stronger demand from India are also supporting prices, it adds. Malaysian Palm Oil Board's February supply and demand data are due afternoon. Technical analysis suggests CPO futures appear bullish, and traders may consider initiating long positions if prices adjust, it adds. AmInvestment Bank sees support for CPO prices at 4,525 ringgit/ton and resistance at 4,688 ringgit/ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery rises 26 ringgit to 4,651 ringgit/ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Standard Chartered Buys Back Over 1.1 Million Shares
data.description:
Standard Chartered repurchased 1,145,000 shares in London on Friday at a volume weighted average price of around 12.26 pounds sterling apiece, a Monday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

The British lender plans to cancel the shares, following which it will have 2,405,184,895 shares in issue.

data.title: European Central Bank's Lagarde and Cipollone could be speaking today
data.description:
ECB President Christine Lagarde and ECB board member Piero Cipollone will be at the Eurogroup meeting in Brussels today, Monday, March 10, 2025.

I wonder if either will get a speaking role, answering questions etc.

Stay tuned.

Lagarde might not say much today

data.title: China's Inflation Data Shows Weakening Demand — Market Talk
data.description: China's February inflation data points to weakening demand in 1Q, Barclays analysts say in a research note. Although the decline in headline CPI was in part distorted by the timing of the Lunar Near Year, negative prints for both core and services CPI point to softer demand, they say. Even after removing the festive distortions, February CPI only rose 0.1%. China's auto sales growth slowed in the first two months compared with 4Q last year despite trade-in subsidies, suggesting "some payback effects" from strong 4Q demand, the bank says. Soft consumption will likely remain a constraint for price recovery, and there aren't any signs of improvement in the labor market entering 2025, Barclays adds. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: HSBC Holdings Buys Back Another 6.1 Million Shares
data.description:
HSBC Holdings bought back 6,093,742 shares in Hong Kong and London on Friday, according to a Monday bourse filing.

The British lender repurchased 3,120,942 shares in the UK at a weighted average price of 8.84 pounds sterling and 2,972,800 shares in Hong Kong at a weighted average price of HK$88.52.

Since the program started, HSBC has repurchased 61,546,877 shares for a total consideration of about $706.8 million.

Upon cancellation of the repurchased shares in the UK, the bank will have 17,795,518,570 ordinary shares.

data.title: India bond yields could inch up as Treasury yields rise, supply hurts
data.description:
Indian government bond yields are expected to inch higher at the start of the week, tracking an uptick in U.S. Treasury yields as well as elevated supply of debt from states.

The benchmark 10-year yield (IN067934G=CC) is likely to move between 6.68% and 6.72%, a trader with a private bank said, compared with its previous close of 6.6881%.

"We could see some pressure on bonds, especially the benchmark yield, which will react to multiple factors which are not so favourable," the trader said.

U.S. yields rose on Friday after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell indicated the central bank could be patient in determining when to cut interest rates.

Powell said the Fed does not need to be in a hurry to cut rates and it remains to be seen whether U.S. President Donald Trump administration's tariff plans will prove to be inflationary, while also listing other factors that could cause price pressures to become more persistent.

Meanwhile, data showed U.S. nonfarm payrolls increased by 151,000 jobs last month, just below the 160,000 estimate of economists polled by Reuters, after rising by a downwardly revised 125,000 in January. The jobless rate ticked up to 4.1% from 4.0% in January.

Interest rate futures are pricing around 75 basis points of rate cuts from the Fed in 2025. It reduced rates by 100 bps in 2024.

Back home, bonds could also witness headwinds as states will sell a larger-than-scheduled quantum of debt this week, their second such move in as many weeks.

They plan to raise 495.22 billion rupees ($5.68 billion) through sale of bonds on Tuesday, nearly 110 billion rupees higher than the pre-announced calendar. Last week, they raised over 100 billion rupees more than scheduled.

India's central bank is set to buy bonds worth 500 billion rupees each on March 12 and March 18, and has not included the benchmark at this week's purchase, which is also weighing on sentiment.

KEY INDICATORS:

** Brent crude futures were down 0.5% at $70 per barrel, after rising 1.3% in the previous session

** Ten-year U.S. Treasury yield at 4.2800%; two-year yield (US2YT=RR) at 3.9811%

** RBI to conduct 1-day variable rate repo auction for 750 billion rupees

($1 = 87.1430 Indian rupees)

data.title: Sidea Semiconductor Equipment Shenzhen Files for Up to 545 Million-Yuan IPO
data.description:
Sidea Semiconductor Equipment Shenzhen (SHE:301629) filed for an initial public offering of up to 10,431,800 shares at 52.28 yuan apiece to raise up to 545 million yuan on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange.

Online subscription date will be on March 11, with payment to follow on March 13, according to a filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Sidea Semiconductor Equipment Shenzhen engages in the research and development of probe testing technology and the mass production of probe stations in China.

data.title: China Stocks Fall on Weak Inflation Data
data.description:
The Shanghai Composite fell 0.4% to below 3,360, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.3% to 10,810 on Monday, extending losses from the previous session as persistent deflationary pressures underscored China's economic challenges.

Data released over the weekend showed that consumer prices fell more than expected in February, hitting a 13-month low.

Meanwhile, producer prices declined for the 29th consecutive month, though at the slowest pace since August 2024.

Beijing recently concluded a key political meeting, where policymakers emphasized their policy toolkit to support economic growth.

However, China’s economic outlook remains clouded by weak domestic demand and an escalating trade war with the US. Notable losses were seen in Talkweb Information (-6%), Zhejiang Huatie (-9.9%), and Hand Enterprise (-7.2%).

data.title: Equatorial Resources Says Final Hearing in International Arbitration Against Congo Postponed
data.description:
Equatorial Resources said the final hearing in its international arbitration against the Republic of Congo at the International Center for Settlement of Investment Disputes, was postponed, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

The postponement came after a request was made to the ICSID arbitral tribunal by the counsel representing Congo saying that Congo had not paid any of the expenses of the hearing including the payment of their counsel.

The counsel representing Congo advised they would not be attending the final hearing for that reason.

The hearing was set to take place in Paris from 10 March to 14 March. The tribunal proposed that the rescheduled final hearing could take place in August or November.

Equatorial, through its Mauritian subsidiary, EEPL Holdings, referred its investment dispute with Congo regarding EEPL's investments in two iron ore projects in Congo, the Badondo project and the Mayoko-Moussondji project, to arbitration in 2021.

data.title: Iron Ore Rises Amid Tight Supply — Market Talk
data.description: Iron ore rises in early Asian trade. Prices fell last week as the market considers cuts to Chinese steel production, ANZ Research analysts write in a note. During the National People's Congress, China's economic planning agency announced that it would mandate steel production cuts, they say. However, losses were limited after the Shanghai Metal Markets reported that iron ore port inventory fell significantly last week, the analysts add. The most-traded iron-ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is up 0.45% at 778.0 yuan a ton.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: CP Axtra Target Price Raised to THB35.00 From THB33.00 by Thanachart Securities >CPAXT.TH
Oil Falls on Weak China Data, Tariff Uncertainty
data.description:
WTI crude oil futures dropped to around $66.6 per barrel on Monday as weak economic data from China and uncertainty over U.S. tariffs fueled demand concerns.

China’s consumer prices fell for the first time in 13 months, while producer price deflation persisted, underscoring deflationary pressures in the world’s largest crude importer.

Oil remains under pressure as President Trump’s shifting tariff policies raised concerns on economic growth and energy demand.

While Trump eased some tariffs on Mexico and Canada until April 2, Canada’s retaliatory tariffs remain in place, and China’s countermeasures are set to take effect later today.

Further weighing on crude, OPEC+ last week agreed to proceed with oil output hikes in April.

However, losses were capped as Trump said the U.S. would increase sanctions on Russia if it fails to reach a ceasefire with Ukraine.

Russia's Deputy Prime Minister Novak also indicated that OPEC+ could reverse the decision in the event of market imbalance.

data.title: Yuan Weakens as Signs of Deflation in China Weighs on Sentiment — Market Talk
data.description: The Chinese yuan weakens against the U.S. dollar in offshore and onshore markets, weighed down by signs of deflation in China. In February, China's CPI fell 0.7% on year, compared with economists' expectations for a 0.5% decline, while the PPI dropped 2.2% on year, against market expectations fore a drop of 2.1%. "It's obvious that domestic demand remains very poor," RBC Capital Markets' Alvin T. Tan says in an email, adding that this will "also be a drag on overall economic growth, and more stimulus measures will be required, including further rate and RRR cuts," the head of Asia FX Strategy says. USD/CNY rises 0.3% to 7.2557; USD/CNH gains 0.2% to 7.2569. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Base Metals Fall, Weighed by Tariff Concerns — Market Talk
data.description: Base metals are broadly lower in early Asian trading, with the three-month LME copper contract down 0.3% at $9,585.00 a ton, aluminum 0.2% lower and nickel off 0.45%. Markets are weighing the impact of duties on U.S. imports of aluminum and steel, as well as the possibility of tariffs on copper, ANZ Research writes in a note. Also, expectations for Chinese stimulus measures have risen after China's CPI declined more than expected in February, it adds. Tariffs could put upward pressure on copper spot prices, given the commodity's relatively low global inventory, though sharp shifts in U.S. trade and foreign policy will likely cause further disruption to metal markets, the research team says in a separate note. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Brent Falls on Weak China Data, Tariff Uncertainty
data.description:
Brent crude oil futures dropped to around $69.9 per barrel on Monday as weak economic data from China and uncertainty over U.S. tariffs fueled demand concerns.

China’s consumer prices fell for the first time in 13 months, while producer price deflation persisted, underscoring deflationary pressures in the world’s largest crude importer.

Oil remains under pressure as President Trump’s shifting tariff policies raised concerns on economic growth and energy demand.

While Trump eased some tariffs on Mexico and Canada until April 2, Canada’s retaliatory tariffs remain in place, and China’s countermeasures are set to take effect later today.

Further weighing on crude, OPEC+ last week agreed to proceed with oil output hikes in April.

However, losses were capped as Trump said the U.S. would increase sanctions on Russia if it fails to reach a ceasefire with Ukraine.

Russia's Deputy Prime Minister Novak also indicated that OPEC+ could reverse the decision in the event of market imbalance.

data.title: Annica Holdings Buys Tire Recycling Plant in Malaysia from GPL Industries
data.description:
Annica Holdings entered into a conditional asset purchase agreement with GPL Industries to acquire certain assets owned by the latter in Tanjung Malim, Perak, Malaysia, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Friday.

Total consideration for the acquisition is SG$2.7 million for the business assets which comprise a waste tire pyrolysis plant.

Meanwhile, the consideration will be satisfied by way of an allotment and issuance of 1,766,666,666 shares in the company's capital at a price of SG$0.0015 per share.

The acquisition is part of the group's strategy to diversify into recycling, renewable energy and green technology business.

data.title: London copper eases on US tariff woes, but weak dollar caps fall
data.description:
London copper edged lower on Monday amid uncertainties around U.S. tariff policies, although some weakness in the dollar limited the downside.

Three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange (LME) eased 0.3% to $9,588 a metric ton by 0223 GMT.

The most-active copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange fell 0.5% to 78,290 yuan ($10,797.73) a ton.

U.S. President Donald Trump in a Fox News interview on Sunday declined to predict whether the U.S. could face a recession amid stock market concerns about his tariff actions on Mexico, Canada and China over fentanyl.

The Trump trade policies have raised trade war fears that could slam economic growth and raise prices for Americans still smarting from years of high inflation.

"Dramatic shifts in U.S. trade and foreign policy are likely to cause further disruption to metal markets," ANZ Research said.

"President Trump has placed a 25% tariff on U.S. aluminium and steel imports. A levy on copper is also on the cards. The short-term impact will be a rise in premiums as the U.S. struggles to adjust to the dislocation in physical markets."

Meanwhile, the dollar began the day weak after significant losses last week due to a potentially weakening U.S. labour market, while concerns over a global trade war led investors to safe havens, lifting the yen and the Swiss franc.

A softer dollar makes greenback-priced commodities less expensive for buyers holding other currencies.

Among other metals, LME aluminium fell 0.5% to $2,692.5 a ton, zinc lost 0.5% to $2,873.5 and nickel eased 0.6% to $16,405. Lead firmed 0.2% to $2,023 and tin eased 0.2% to $32,455.

SHFE aluminium shed 0.2% to 20,845 yuan a ton, zinc lost 0.9% to 23,845 yuan and lead slid 0.3% to 17,430 yuan. Nickel climbed 1.3% to 131,970 yuan and tin firmed 0.08% to 262,150 yuan.

($1 = 7.2506 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Japan futures hit near 4-month low on firmer yen, China deflationary worries
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures fell for a fifth straight session on Monday as the yen strengthened, while deflationary pressures in top consumer China fueled growth concerns.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for August delivery , was down 7.4 yen, or 2.12%, at 342.3 yen ($2.32) per kg as of 0209 GMT.

  • The contract fell to 341.3 yen earlier in the session, its lowest since November 14.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for May delivery fell 245 yuan, or 1.4%, to 17,230 yuan ($2,376.36) per metric ton.

  • The most-active April butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) shed 110 yuan, or 0.8%, to 13,585 yuan ($1,873.64) per metric ton.

  • China's consumer price index in February missed expectations and fell at the sharpest pace in 13 months, while producer price deflation persisted, as households remain cautious about spending.

  • The country's imports unexpectedly shrank over the January-February period, while exports lost momentum, amid escalating tariff pressures from the United States.

  • Meanwhile, risk-averse investors sought the Japanese yen , with the currency 0.5% firmer at 147.27 per dollar, just shy of a five-month high.

  • A stronger yen makes assets denominated in the currency less affordable to overseas buyers.

  • Oil prices fell on Monday as concern about the impact of U.S. import tariffs on global economic growth and fuel demand, as well as rising output from OPEC+ producers, cooled investor appetite for riskier assets.

  • Natural rubber often takes direction from oil prices as it competes for market share with synthetic rubber, which is made from crude oil.

  • Farmers should be wary of crop damage from March 10-15, said top rubber producer Thailand's meteorological agency, adding that the South would face thundershowers and isolated heavy rains.

  • The front-month rubber contract on Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for March delivery last traded at 198.3 U.S. cents per kg, down 0.7%.

($1 = 147.3300 yen)

($1 = 7.2506 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Chifeng Jilong Gold Mining Makes Tepid Hong Kong Debut as Shares Open Flat
data.description:
Chifeng Jilong Gold Mining , witnessed a tepid stock market debut as its shares opened flat on their maiden trading in Hong Kong Monday morning.

The gold mining and selling company opened at HK$13.72 per share, unchanged from its IPO price.

In Shanghai trading Monday morning, Chifeng Jilong Gold Mining's shares fell more than 1%.

data.title: HSBC to Redeem $2 Billion Bonds Due 2026
data.description:
HSBC Holdings will redeem all the $2 billion 1.645% fixed rate/floating rate senior unsecured bonds due 2026, which the lender issued on Aug. 26, 2009, according to a Monday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The British lender will redeem the securities on April 18 at $1,000 for each equivalent principal amount of the bonds.

All accrued but unsettled interest from Oct. 18, 2024, until the redemption date will be paid to security holders on record as of April 3, 2025.

data.title: Banking Central |How far could the RBI go on the liquidity mission?
data.description:
Last week, speaking at the Moneycontrol Global wealth summit, one of the panellists said the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) may have to do more on its part to spur the economic growth by infusing additional funds into the financial system. That echoes well with the pro-growth sentiments at the reconstituted monetary policy committee (MPC) headed by the new central bank governor Sanjay Malhotra. It looks like the RBI will continue to pump in more money to bridge the liquidity shortage in the system and thereby positively influence the credit growth to the productive sectors.

In fact, ever since Malhotra took over as the RBI Governor, the central bank’s stance has unmistakably shifted towards supporting growth. The dogged focus on retail inflation that defined RBI policy for over two years is now making way for a more flexible approach. Liquidity, once scarce, is now flowing back into the system in massive volumes.

The numbers tell the story. Since Malhotra’s appointment on December 11, 2024, the RBI has pumped around Rs 51.28 lakh crore into the banking system through an aggressive mix of short-term and durable liquidity measures. This includes Rs 18.27 lakh crore in normal variable rate repo (VRR) auctions, Rs 31.56 lakh crore in daily VRR auctions, Rs 1 lakh crore in open market purchases, and a foreign currency buy-sell swap worth Rs 1.3 lakh crore.

 Banking Central

Liquidity shortages have fluctuated wildly—from Rs 30,000 crore to Rs 3 lakh crore on different days between mid-December and mid-February. While the deficit has now eased to Rs 54,000 crore as of March 5, the RBI is not taking any chances. It has announced fresh open market operations (OMO) worth Rs 1 lakh crore in two tranches (March 12 and March 18) and a USD/INR Buy/Sell Swap auction worth $10 billion on March 24.

These moves are both strategic and urgent, given the fragility of the current financial landscape.

By opting for staggered OMO auctions, the RBI is not just replenishing bank reserves but also sending a clear signal—it will act decisively to maintain stability. The stakes are high. The current liquidity crunch stems from multiple factors: global economic uncertainties, domestic credit pressures, and external market shocks. Left unchecked, these could snowball into a systemic financial crisis. The planned swap auction is another critical step to counter currency volatility, which, if mismanaged, could amplify domestic liquidity constraints.

However, the question is whether what it has done on the liquidity front already is enough? As mentioned above, experts already pitch for an additional injection of Rs 2-3 lakh crore into the banking system to truly support growth. The flip side? A fresh surge in liquidity could stoke inflationary pressures, undoing the RBI’s hard-won price stability.

So, will the central bank keep the liquidity taps open risking a resurgence in the inflation? That's a tough call to make.(Banking Central is a weekly column that keeps a close watch on and connects the dots regarding the sector's most important events for readers.)

data.title: Audalia Resources Posts Wider Fiscal H1 Loss
data.description:
Audalia Resources reported Monday a fiscal first-half loss of AU$0.0008 per diluted share, wider compared with a loss of AU$0.0006 a year earlier.

Other income for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$4,026, compared with AU$2,417 a year earlier.

data.title: Shanghai Turbo Enterprises to Make Further Investment in Sichuan 3D
data.description:
Shanghai Turbo Enterprises outlined its intention to inject a further 3 million yuan in Sichuan 3D as part of its fourth phase of investment, according to a Friday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The total amount of investment in the wholly owned subsidiary is now 13 million yuan.

The investment will allow the company to implement business activities and improve overall operational efficiency.

data.title: RailTel Corporation shares in focus on order win worth Rs 28.29 crore; board meet on March 12
data.description:
RailTel Corporation of India share price likely to extend gains on fifth consecutive day on March 10 following the company received the work order from Northern Railway amounting to Rs 28,29,14,088.

The order consists indoor and outdoor double distant signalling work, to be completed by September 6, 2026.

A meeting of the board of directors of the company is scheduled to be held on March 12, 2025, to consider and declare second interim dividend, if any, for the Financial Year 2024-25 and to fix the record date to ascertain the shareholders who are eligible for the interim dividend.

Catch all the market action on our live blog

In the previous week, company received the work order from State Transport Authority Odisha for project business amounting to Rs 2,62,33,85,540 for upgradation of existing Command Control Centre to Unified Command Control Centre & Implementation of Intelligent Enforcement Management System (lEMS) for State Transport Authority.

Also, the company received the work order from Border Security Force for an order amounting to Rs 19,08,82,886 and work order from East Central Railway for works amounting to Rs 47,50,05,370.

In the quarter ended December 2024, the company posted nearly 5 percent on year growth in net profit to Rs 65 crore, up from Rs 62.10 crore in the year ago period. Revenue jumped 14.8 percent year-on-year to Rs 767.6 crore, as against Rs 668.4 crore in Q3 FY24.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
